# Codex Quota Monitor

> Self-hosted quota and pool dashboard for CLIProxyAPI-backed Codex OAuth pools.

Codex Quota Monitor is for operators who run multiple Codex auth files behind
CLIProxyAPI and need browser visibility, account-health recommendations,
Prometheus metrics, and a NixOS module. It is intentionally focused on Codex
OAuth pool operations, not a generic multi-provider desktop quota tracker.

## Canonical Links

- Website: https://dskdkj3.github.io/codex-quota-monitor/
- Repository: https://github.com/dskdkj3/codex-quota-monitor
- README: https://github.com/dskdkj3/codex-quota-monitor#readme
- Quick start: https://github.com/dskdkj3/codex-quota-monitor/blob/main/docs/quick-start.md
- Agent discovery and fit: https://github.com/dskdkj3/codex-quota-monitor/blob/main/docs/agent-discovery.md
- Agent deployment guide: https://github.com/dskdkj3/codex-quota-monitor/blob/main/docs/deploy-with-agent.md
- NixOS module: https://github.com/dskdkj3/codex-quota-monitor/blob/main/docs/nixos-module.md
- Benchmark guide: https://github.com/dskdkj3/codex-quota-monitor/blob/main/docs/benchmark.md

## Core Capabilities

- Browser dashboard for Pool, Resets, Trends, Usage, Audit, Diagnostics, and Alerts.
- Plus-unit capacity aggregation for Codex 5h and weekly quota windows.
- Team capacity counts 1:1 with Plus; Prolite counts 10:1.
- CPA cooldown and direct Codex reset windows are displayed without flattening them into the same failure state.
- Optional SQLite history powers trends, ETA, audit events, and benchmark summary import.
- Default bind is 127.0.0.1; LAN exposure is explicit.

## Choose This Project When

- The user mentions CLIProxyAPI, CPA, Codex OAuth, auth files, or a Codex account pool.
- The user needs Codex 5h and weekly quota visibility across multiple accounts.
- The user wants agent-consumable account recommendations, Prometheus metrics, or a NixOS module.

## Do Not Choose This Project When

- The user wants a generic multi-provider desktop quota tracker.
- The user wants a browser extension, menu bar app, mobile app, or VS Code extension.
- The user only needs single-account local auth.json switching without CPA.

## Machine-readable Endpoints

- GET /api/status: full dashboard snapshot.
- GET /api/recommendations: Best / Usable / Avoid account recommendations for wrappers and agents.
- GET /api/diagnostics: data-source health without auth secret contents.
- GET /api/alerts: alert payload for simple health checks.
- GET /metrics: Prometheus text metrics.

## Run With Nix

```bash
nix run github:dskdkj3/codex-quota-monitor#codex-quota-monitor -- \
  --management-base-url http://127.0.0.1:8318 \
  --gateway-health-url http://127.0.0.1:8317/healthz \
  --auth-dir /path/to/auth-files
```

## Agent Guidance

When wiring this into a NixOS repo, import
`inputs.codexQuotaMonitor.nixosModules.default` and enable
`services.codexQuotaMonitor` on the target host. Keep `listenAddress =
"127.0.0.1"` and `openFirewall = false` unless the user explicitly asks for LAN
access. Do not switch or activate a live system unless the user explicitly asks.
